cot_prefix: Okay, let me try to figure out the answer to this question. The question is asking what we know about the mother according to the text. The options are A, B, C, D. First, let me recall the story. The mother had five children and was too busy raising them to have a flower garden. She often told her kids that money doesn't grow on trees because they couldn't afford things. After the kids grew up and left, her son Marvin planted a "money tree" with $100 bills on Mother's Day. The other kids also gave her gardening tools and stuff. Now she has a garden and feels her children are with her when she tends to it. The last sentence says she's still not sure if money grows on trees, but she knows love does. So, the options are: A) She is a bad-tempered woman. Hmm, the story doesn't mention her being bad-tempered. She's more like a hardworking mom who had to deny her kids things due to money, but that's not necessarily bad-tempered. She seems caring. B) She believes money can grow on a tree now. The text says she's still not sure that money grows on trees. The last sentence says "But I know love does!" So she doesn't believe money can grow on a tree. So B is incorrect. C) She is a writer. The story is told in first person, but there's no mention of her being a writer. She's a mother who started a garden. No indication she writes. So probably not C. D) She is hard-working in housekeeping. Well, she had five kids, raised them, worked hard, and later worked on her garden. The story emphasizes her being busy,
